text:
Once upon a time, there was a frog who dreamed about flying. Every night, he sat beside his tranquil pond and whispered his wish aloud. Dawn came, and he stretched his legs and jumped. His legs turned into wings. He flapped harder, and flew!

He soared above trees and rivers. Birds twittered below him. He felt free. He couldn’t believe he had turned his dream into reality.

Time passed. He became famous. Every morning people would look up to see him soaring high. Some worried he might hurt himself. Others thought it strange.

One day a wise old owl visited him. “Fly high,” he said. “But remember,” paused. “Only fly when you feel like it.”

The frog flew higher every chance he got. Seasons changed. Winter froze him. He curled up on a nearby stump but dreamed on.

Next spring, he woke to find eggs. Frogs don’t lay eggs. He was stuck. He realized he’d missed flying. But
